error_message: Deploy preflight takes 5-6 min per invocation; PHP lint walks vendor/
root_cause: find at line 206 walked all .php under THEME_DIR with no prunes. 3,881 files total but only 124 are the theme's; the other 3,756 are Composer vendor deps. At 0.36s per php -l, vendor-lint alone cost 5-6 min per deploy. vendor/ was already gitignored, excluded from tarball, and excluded from phpcs — the lint walk was the last path that didn't get the memo.
fix: Added -prune for vendor, node_modules, tests, .git. Replaced hardcoded /opt/homebrew/bin/php with `command -v php` fallback. Added file count to success log. Scope: 124 (was 3,881). Expected preflight: ~45s (was ~5-6 min).

error_message: No concurrency guard; two deploys can race on the remote .old.$swap_id rename
root_cause: Hot-swap uses a timestamp+PID swap_id for rename-aside-and-rename-into-place. Two parallel deploys produce different swap_ids but race on the same live path. If deploy B's rename lands between A's rename-aside and rename-into-place, A loses its backup anchor and B's backup points at A's half-extracted tarball.
fix: Added acquire_deploy_lock() using /tmp/skyyrose-deploy.lock with PID stored inside. Stale lock (PID dead) reclaimed; live lock refuses second deploy with exit 2. release_deploy_lock fires in cleanup trap.

error_message: If verify_live() fails, site stays broken; no auto-rollback
root_cause: verify_live returned non-zero but main() only logged and exited 1 — the broken theme remained live. Old theme was already deleted by the immediate rm -rf .old.$swap_id at end of try_rsync, so no backup to restore.
fix: Added auto_rollback() that SSHs remote, reverses the swap (mv broken to .failed.$ts, restore .old.$ts into live path), and flushes caches. Changed try_rsync to NOT delete .old.$ts immediately. Cleanup trap inspects ROLLBACK_REQUESTED and calls auto_rollback on verify failure. Exit code 3 signals rollback occurred.

error_message: .old.$swap_id deleted immediately after swap; no rollback anchor
root_cause: Original compound command chained `mv new -> live && rm -rf .old.$swap_id`. Bounded disk but destroyed the only artifact auto-rollback could restore from.
fix: Removed the immediate rm. Added deterministic retention: after every swap, ls .old.* on remote and xargs rm -rf all but the 2 most recent. Bounded disk, rollback anchor preserved.

error_message: verify_live checked only homepage; CSS/JS breakage silent when WP-CDN cached HTML
root_cause: verify_live() only curled the homepage. If WP-CDN served cached HTML but new CSS/JS 404'd from origin, check passed while site was visibly broken. Also: no per-phase timing, no structured log.
fix: Extended verify_live() to curl style.css, immersive.css, immersive.js with cache-busting. Every asset URL must return 200. Added DEPLOY_CHECK_ASSETS env var for project-specific additions. Added phase_start/phase_end/phase_summary (bash 3.2 compatible scalars — no associative arrays). Tee'd stdout+stderr to /tmp/skyyrose-deploy-<ts>.log.

error_message: tar -czf wastes CPU compressing already-compressed media (WebP/GLB/JPG/PNG)
root_cause: Theme payload ~380 MB, of which ~316 MB is already-compressed binary media. gzip achieves ~0% reduction on those formats but costs single-threaded CPU per deploy.
fix: Runtime-detect tar --zstd and zstd binary. When available, use `tar --zstd -cf` (5x faster, similar ratio on text parts). Fallback to plain `-cf` (no compression) when zstd absent — still faster than gzip on pre-compressed media. Matching remote extract flag. Tarball extension adjusts.

error_message: SSHPASS exported for entire deploy duration, leaking credential to every child process
root_cause: build_ssh_cmd() unconditionally exported SSHPASS as soon as the script started; remained in env for preflight, tar, every subprocess until exit. On shared systems, `ps e <pid>` or /proc/<pid>/environ leak the password.
fix: Moved SSHPASS export from build_ssh_cmd() into try_rsync() just before scp. Added `unset SSHPASS` immediately after remote extract. Exposure window shrunk from entire deploy (~7 min) to sub-second. No functional change — sshpass -e reads from env at invocation.

error_message: 43.4 MB orphan GIF bloating every deploy payload for no reason
root_cause: GIF (1920x1080, 326 frames) superseded by rose-gold-spin.mp4 (3.86 MB) and rose-gold-spin.webm (2.34 MB) on 2026-04-14 but source GIF never removed. Zero references anywhere in theme PHP/CSS/JS/JSON. Every deploy paid the tar + upload cost for a file that would never be served.
fix: Pre-delete grep confirmed zero references. Deleted the .gif. MP4/WebM remain as modern animated-logo assets. Savings: 43.4 MB off every future tarball (11% payload reduction).

error_message: template-preorder-gateway.php: (a) missing kids-capsule collection, (b) $fallback_products uses nonexistent SKUs (br-hoodie, lh-hoodie, sg-hoodie etc.), (c) wc_get_products() query with fallback to fake data, (d) card args missing image_url and correct permalink

error_message: TypeError: e.target.closest is not a function — SVG elements and other non-Element nodes dispatched to document-level event listeners don't have .closest()
root_cause: SVG child elements (path, circle, use, etc.) are SVGElement not HTMLElement and may not implement .closest() in older browsers. document-level capture listeners receive events from any element on the page including SVG internals.
fix: Added typeof guard before every .closest() call: getCardData(), mouseenter listener, mouseleave listener, click listener — each now checks !el || typeof el.closest !== 'function' before calling.

fix: Replaced both references (path and URL) to skyyrose-mascot-reference.png with skyy-canonical.jpeg in the mascot fallback block of 404.php.

error_message: Tripo generate_multiview_image hallucinated brand canon on 30 SKUs (120 renders) — invented 'rose-on-cloud' motif on br-001, rendered br-011 hockey jersey as cyan/teal hoodie with invented crest, applied off-canon patch to wrong location on sg-007 signature beanie
root_cause: Tripo `generate_multiview_image` template runs FLUX.1 Kontext with naked image-only input — accepts NO prompt, NO logo overlay, NO dossier branding spec, NO palette token. With no canon anchor, FLUX defaults to training-set priors. Dispatch boundary had no guard distinguishing branded SKUs (which Tripo cannot render correctly) from unbranded clean tech-flats (which it can). Additionally, SKUs with empty catalog `image` column silently fell back to `front_model_image` (a model-on shot), which the template re-builds from scratch — changing garment type entirely (jersey → hoodie). No QA gate caught the regression; STOP-AND-SHOW manifest showed cost only, no output preview.
fix: scripts/tripo_dispatch.py — added classify_skus() function that blocks at the dispatch boundary: (1) SKUs whose dossier frontmatter has `logo_reference:` populated are blocked as BRANDED with directive to route via ADK render_pipeline. (2) SKUs with empty `image` column or missing tech-flat file are blocked as NO TECH-FLAT. --force-branded escape hatch added with WARNING and still-required explicit y to dispatch. Per-SKU block reason printed in manifest before y/N prompt. 12 unit tests in tests/test_tripo_dispatch_guards.py covering both guards plus escape hatch. renders/output/tripo/QUARANTINE.md written marking all 120 existing outputs as discard-do-not-publish. Cerebrum Do-Not-Repeat entry added 2026-05-11.

error_message: CURS-03: luxury cursor JS loads on immersive templates (slug='immersive') — no exclusion gate in enqueue.php
root_cause: wp_enqueue_script('skyyrose-luxury-cursor', ...) lives inside skyyrose_enqueue_global_scripts() at lines 249-259 with no conditional check. Template slug detection exists (skyyrose_get_current_template_slug() maps template-immersive-*.php to 'immersive') but is not consulted before cursor JS enqueue. Result: cursor JS loads on all pages including immersive templates where the cursor is CSS-hidden.
fix: FIXED in theme 1.1.2 (commit 016f7025f, deployed 2026-05-12). wordpress-theme/skyyrose-flagship/inc/enqueue.php lines 247-263: wrapped the luxury-cursor wp_enqueue_script() block in `if ('immersive' !== skyyrose_get_current_template_slug())`. The template slug helper was already available in the same file (no new dependency). After deploy, live verification confirmed zero <script> tags loading luxury-cursor.js on /experience-black-rose/. Regression test tests/test_luxury_cursor.py::test_cursor_not_loaded_on_immersive flipped from intentional-fail to pass (3/3 green).

error_message: DATA-01: /collection-black-rose/, /collection-love-hurts/, /collection-signature/, /collection-kids-capsule/ all returned HTTP 200 but served the homepage (page-id-9822, page-template-front-page-php) instead of the matching template-collection-*.php. Collection pages were absent from the WordPress DB.
root_cause: skyyrose_run_activation_setup() runs once via add_action('init', ...) at priority 30, gated by `if get_option('skyyrose_activation_setup_version') === SKYYROSE_SETUP_VERSION return;`. The stored option was '4.0.0', matching the constant, so the init runner short-circuited on every request. However, the four collection pages had been deleted from the WP admin at some point (or never created on this site's initial activation), so skyyrose_create_required_pages() never re-ran to recreate them. wp_safe_redirect logic in inc/redirects.php only handled legacy /collections/{slug}/ -> /collection-{slug}/ rewrites — it assumed the canonical destination pages existed, which they did not.
fix: Bumped SKYYROSE_SETUP_VERSION constant from '4.0.0' to '4.1.0' in inc/theme-activation-setup.php (commit 016f7025f, theme 1.1.2). On next request after deploy the init runner sees the version mismatch, fires skyyrose_run_activation_setup(), which calls skyyrose_create_required_pages(). That function iterates the slug map (already includes the four collection slugs at lines 69-88) and runs wp_insert_post() for any slug returned as missing by get_page_by_path(), assigning _wp_page_template meta to point at the matching template-collection-*.php. Post-deploy verified live: /collection-black-rose/ = page-id-9454, love-hurts=9455, signature=9456, kids-capsule=9651 — all routing to their correct templates.
regression_test: scripts/verify_live_structure.py COLLECTION_HERO_ASSETS asserts img[src*='sr-collection-*.webp'] per collection

error_message: Stop hook error: Hook JSON output validation failed — (root): Invalid input
root_cause: Stop hook emitted {hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext: ...} JSON. That schema is valid only for UserPromptSubmit and PostToolUse. Stop accepts only {decision, reason, continue, stopReason, suppressOutput, systemMessage}. Claude Code schema validator rejected the unknown root-level shape.
fix: Replaced jq stdout JSON emission with stderr printf. Stop fires after Claude finishes, so additionalContext injection is moot — the reminder is for the human, stderr is the right channel. Stdout now empty, schema validator passes. Marker file deletion preserved.

error_message: NOT SUPPORTED: option missingRefs. Pass empty schema with $id that should be ignored to ajv.addSchema. TypeError: Cannot set properties of undefined (setting 'defaultMeta') at ajvOrig
root_cause: ESLint 9.39.3 bundles ajv that rejects the 'missingRefs' option used by @eslint/eslintrc's legacy config loader. Lint-staged invokes ESLint 9 which loads eslintrc-universal.cjs which calls ajvOrig with NOT-SUPPORTED options -> hard crash before any file is linted. Blocks pre-commit on any staged .ts/.tsx under frontend/**
fix: Scoped npm overrides in root package.json: 'eslint': {'ajv': '^6.14.0'} AND '@eslint/eslintrc': {'ajv': '^6.14.0'}. Forces both packages to nest ajv v6.15.0 in their own node_modules while keeping top-level ajv@8.18.0 for other consumers. Verified: 'cd frontend && npx eslint <file>' runs to completion (2 warnings, 0 errors, exit 0). Fixed in chore/p1-p5-backlog at TBD-commit.

error_message: error: 'tuple' object is not callable (TestModules in tests/aos/shell/test_commands.py)
root_cause: ModuleRegistry.manifests and .registered_types are @property accessors, not methods; _cmd_modules called them with () which invoked the returned tuple/frozenset.
fix: Dropped the parentheses: kernel.modules.manifests / kernel.modules.registered_types accessed as properties.

error_message: Phase 14 INFRA-01 regression: scripts/nano_banana/catalog.py bypasses skyyrose.core.catalog_loader (independent csv.DictReader) — single-source-of-truth contract broken; Plan 14-02 SUMMARY claim false
root_cause: Plan 14-02 added a catalog_loader shim (commit 51dc3ff8b). Later commits a22074ab3 and 8737e3714 overwrote scripts/nano_banana/catalog.py with a standalone csv.DictReader parser (lines 8, 26-55), regressing INFRA-01. Data stays consistent (same canonical CSV file) but the three pipelines no longer share the catalog adapter — the contract, not the data, is violated.
fix: Option A applied. Rewrote scripts/nano_banana/catalog.py load_catalog() + module docstring + CATALOG_CSV constant to source rows via skyyrose.core.catalog_loader.read_catalog_rows / bool_col / CATALOG_CSV (env-overridable). Removed the local csv.DictReader. Preserved the richer HEAD functions untouched (find_source_image, find_back_source, load_products, load_specs, get_material_spec — load-bearing per cli.py/produce_async.py) and kept engine_override + back_source_override; added garment_type from garment_type_lock (INFRA-04 alignment). Added CI regression gate tests/test_catalog_csv_integrity.py::test_nano_banana_catalog_routes_through_core_loader asserting nb.read_catalog_rows IS core.read_catalog_rows, canonical CATALOG_CSV, and no csv.DictReader in load_catalog. Verified: 21/21 integrity tests green incl. test_python_loaders_agree_on_sku_set; nano+elite agree on 33 SKUs; black/ruff/isort clean.

error_message: 5 prod modules reference data/product-catalog.csv as 'single source of truth' but that file is NOT on the main tree and NOT git-tracked (only the canonical wordpress-theme/skyyrose-flagship/data/skyyrose-catalog.csv is tracked)
root_cause: Legacy second-CSV references predating the catalog_loader consolidation. error_message: (eval):17: PIPESTATUS[0]: parameter not set  — verification harness for devworkflows scripts failed under set -u
file: scripts/devworkflows/ (verification harness, not the scripts themselves)
root_cause: The Bash tool executes commands via zsh (env Shell: zsh), not bash. PIPESTATUS is a bash-only array; zsh exposes pipe exit codes as the lowercase 1-indexed `pipestatus` array. With `set -u`, referencing the unset PIPESTATUS made the harness abort. The 3 devworkflow scripts were unaffected (they run under their own `#!/usr/bin/env bash` shebang) — only the inline test harness tripped.
fix: In Bash-tool one-liners, never use ${PIPESTATUS[*]}. Capture exit status directly: run the command, then `rc=$?` on the next line (no pipe), or redirect to a file and read $? immediately. Reserve PIPESTATUS for code inside `bash script.sh` files.

error_message: AssertionError: assert '' == 'skyyrose-assets' — tests/services/test_r2_client.py::TestR2Config::test_default_bucket (full-suite only)
root_cause: Per-domain .env scaffold emitted unfilled keys as bare 'KEY='. skyyrose.core.env_loader.load_project_env lists skyyrose/.env in its load order; load_dotenv(override=False) still SETS a key not already present, so 'KEY=' injected an explicit empty string into global os.environ. r2_client's os.getenv('R2_BUCKET', DEFAULT_BUCKET) then returned '' instead of 'skyyrose-assets', polluting full-suite test ordering. error_message: Adversarial review of the new deterministic fraud scorer (services/risk/fraud.py) found 16 real defects. Top: (HIGH) never-raises contract broken — `raw = sum(s.weight for s in signals)` sat OUTSIDE the try/except, so FraudScorer(weights={'x':'str'}).assess(...) raised TypeError; (HIGH) false-positive — a legit first-time guest sending a $500+ gift internationally stacked country(20)+name(10)+highvalue(10)+firsttime(15)=55 -> HIGH/review, costing real sales; (MEDIUM) mixed naive/aware date_created silently zeroed ALL velocity signals (TypeError caught -> every history row out-of-window); (MEDIUM) raw email/IP/full names leaked into signal detail strings + as_dict; (MEDIUM) markdown pipe-injection in format_assessment; (LOW) NaN total treated as 0-risk; current order counted in its own velocity; guest==None false-positive; stale hardcoded total_tools.

Added 9 regression tests.

error_message: Adversarial review of the 3 remaining Tier-2 scorers (retention/forecasting/personalization) found 43 confirmed findings (6 HIGH/13 MED/24 LOW; heavy dedup -> ~16 distinct). Top: (HIGH x3, same root) LOYAL lifecycle stage UNREACHABLE past at_risk_days=90 because recency gates fired before the loyalty frequency check -> a 5-order buyer in a normal 91-180d inter-drop gap was demoted to AT_RISK and routed to win-back (100% false-positive on loyal buyers for SkyyRose's quarterly drop cadence); (HIGH) pre-order products triggered false CRITICAL 'reorder_now' alerts because is_preorder was captured but never consulted in sellout risk; (HIGH) personalization popularity used raw unbounded count -> a bestseller could swamp personalized signals; (MED) forecast window included today (partial day) inflating velocity; forecast_units_30d used self.horizon_days not literal 30; _price used `or` dropping price=0.0; segment_customers/forecast_sales fallbacks called now() per row (non-deterministic batch); _parse_dt dropped datetime objects; (HIGH, verified-but-out-of-scope) orphaned analytics_forecast_sales ToolSpec -> documented canonical surface rather than building dispatch.
root_cause: Green TDD proved each rule fires in isolation but never tested cross-band interactions (stage-ordering precedence), domain calibration (drop cadence, pre-order semantics), bounded scales (raw-count swamp), batch determinism, or real-world datetime types. Adversarial review with domain context surfaced them.
fix: Retention: loyalty check moved before the at_risk recency gate so loyal buyers stay LOYAL through the inter-drop window (degrade only past dormant/churned); churn_risk=0 when frequency<=0; _parse_dt accepts datetime. Forecasting: is_preorder -> SelloutRisk.NONE; window excludes today (recency>=1); forecast_units_30d fixed at literal 30d (removed dead horizon_days ctor param); removed unreachable _trend branch; _parse_dt accepts datetime. Personalization: popularity log1p-scaled; _price uses explicit None; added similarity_k fanout param. analytics_agent: forecast_sales/segment_customers take as_of (single now -> reproducible batch), segment_customers rejects non-rfm methods gracefully, ToolSpec metadata documented. Added 15 regression tests (148 total green).

error_message: Commit 6eab086ed (brand-learning ApprovalGate fix) orphaned — not in HEAD ancestry, changes gone from working tree

error_message: Cleanup classifier marked all 81 untracked files as 'on main, differs' (KEEP=0) — would have recommended deleting 76 genuine local files = data loss
root_cause: Used `git rev-parse origin/main:$f` as an existence test. For a path NOT in the tree, git rev-parse ECHOES the unparseable string to stdout (errors on stderr) instead of returning empty, so every file looked present. Real blob hash never matched the echoed string -> universal false 'differs'.
fix: Use `git cat-file -e origin/main:$f` (or `git ls-tree`) for path existence — does not echo. Re-classified: only 4 (deleted) + regen-boost (deleted) were truly on main; 76 are genuine local work, kept.

error_message: Turbopack build failed: Route segment config "dynamic" is not compatible with nextConfig.cacheComponents. Please remove it.

error_message: Build error: Both middleware file "./middleware.ts" and proxy file "./proxy.ts" are detected. Please use "./proxy.ts" only.

error_message: All /api/v1/training/* endpoints returned HTTP 500 (coroutine '_find_training_versions' was never awaited; TypeError 'coroutine' object is not iterable). After await fix: ValueError: Out of range float values are not JSON compliant: inf
root_cause: A prior perf refactor (a16ee1d67) converted the file helpers to async (aiofiles/asyncio.to_thread) but left every call site synchronous -> each endpoint received a coroutine object (truthy), so guards passed and iteration/attr-access raised -> caught -> 500. Bug-behind-a-bug: TrainingProgressResponse.best_loss defaulted to float('inf'), which is not JSON-compliant; the await bug masked it because requests never reached serialization. imagery/training_progress_reporter.py writes real progress.json with best_loss=Infinity, so live data hits this too.
fix: Added 'await' to all 9 helper call sites (_find_training_versions/_load_progress_file/_load_status_file/_load_round_table_results). Made best_loss Optional[float] default None + field_validator coercing non-finite (inf/-inf/nan) -> None. Also wired POST /training/export to real huggingface_hub HfApi.create_repo+upload_file (dataset) via asyncio.to_thread, honest fallback: missing HF_TOKEN -> local-only success with dataset_url=None; HF failure -> 502; real URL only from a real RepoUrl. 13 tests added (tests/api/test_training_status.py). Dashboard get_training_status() consumer was also repaired by this fix.

error_message: model-viewer GLB load times out in browser; only 8/33 models render (glb-models.html)
root_cause: Two compounding causes: (1) meshopt-compressed GLBs need the meshopt decoder, which model-viewer only loads when meshoptDecoderLocation is configured BEFORE the first <model-viewer> element is created; (2) opening the page via file:// blocks fetch() of the GLBs and WASM workers (Chrome CORS on file:// origin), so nothing loads regardless of decoder config.
fix: Configure self.ModelViewerElement.meshoptDecoderLocation/ktx2TranscoderLocation/dracoDecoderLocation to vendored local paths (renders/3d/_viewer/) in a <script> placed before the model-viewer module <script> (lines 4-9), and serve the page over HTTP from the repo root (python3 -m http.server) instead of file://. Verified via Playwright: 33/33 model-viewers loaded, 0 load errors after scroll; geometry renders correctly (br-001 crewneck, br-002 joggers, br-003 jersey, etc.). The 8/33 initial count is loading="lazy" working as designed (IntersectionObserver), not a failure.

error_message: ImportError: cannot import name 'WordPressWooCommerceClient' from 'integrations.wordpress_client' — CommerceAgent WooCommerce integration is dead on main (WORDPRESS_AVAILABLE=False), so commerce/products/bulk returns per-product 'client not connected' at runtime even though the wire is correct.
root_cause: Pre-existing agent<->client interface mismatch. agents/commerce_agent.py:48 does `from integrations.wordpress_client import WordPressWooCommerceClient` (try/except ImportError -> WORDPRESS_AVAILABLE=False fallback at line 53), but integrations/wordpress_client.py defines the class as WordPressClient (no WordPressWooCommerceClient alias). Verified via `python -c import`: ImportError + WORDPRESS_AVAILABLE=False + client=None. Additionally CommerceAgent._ensure_wordpress_client calls `await client.connect()`, but WordPressClient has no connect() — it exposes an async context-manager API (__aenter__/__aexit__, close()). So even fixing the name, the connection lifecycle is wrong. Discovered while wiring T3-2 commerce/products/bulk; the 3 wire tests mock the agent so they pass while the real integration is non-functional. api/dashboard.py:811-917 imports the same broken name.
fix: FIXED 2026-06-28 (founder approved full fix). (1) integrations/wordpress_client.py: added module-level alias `WordPressWooCommerceClient = WordPressClient` + a `WordPressClient.from_env()` classmethod (reads WORDPRESS_URL/WP_SITE_URL + WC_CONSUMER_KEY/WOOCOMMERCE_KEY + WC_CONSUMER_SECRET/WOOCOMMERCE_SECRET; raises ValueError when missing). (2) agents/commerce_agent.py _ensure_wordpress_client: dropped the non-existent `.connect()`, now constructs via `WordPressWooCommerceClient.from_env()` (honors an injected client, degrades gracefully on missing creds keeping _wordpress_connected=False). (3) api/dashboard.py: 3 sites swapped `WordPressWooCommerceClient()` -> `.from_env()`. Verified: import resolves (is WordPressClient True), WORDPRESS_AVAILABLE flipped False->True, from_env graceful-raise on missing creds; black+ruff clean on all 3 files; rtk proxy pytest tests/api/test_commerce_bulk_wire.py + tests/test_core_agents.py = 231 passed (no regression). optimize_price (in main) uses ml_module not _ensure_wordpress_client, so unaffected.

error_message: Stop-hook RED locally: tests/mcp/test_wc_client.py::test_missing_env_raises_keyerror DID NOT RAISE KeyError. Local-only — CI was green.
root_cause: Follow-on to bug-165's WC unification (commit 7426334dc). WCCredentials.from_env() called a helper _ensure_wordpress_env_loaded() that load_dotenv'd .env.wordpress BEFORE reading os.environ. On a dev machine .env.wordpress exists, so the auto-load repopulated the exact credential vars the test had just cleared via monkeypatch.delenv -> from_env resolved creds -> KeyError never raised -> test failed. In CI .env.wordpress is gitignored/absent, so the load no-op'd and the test passed: a test whose outcome depends on an untracked file's presence is non-deterministic. A parallel session compounded confusion by editing the same files (kept auto-load + stubbed the helper in a new test) while this session removed the auto-load.
fix: FIXED 2026-06-29, committed 7bfce75cf direct to main (founder: 'commit the wc fix to main'). (1) wc_safe_client.py: removed _ensure_wordpress_env_loaded entirely; from_env() now reads os.environ ONLY (pure), so callers/tests fully control the env via monkeypatch. (2) devskyy_mcp.py: the application bootstrap now does load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent/'.env.wordpress') after imports (with noqa: E402 on the post-load mcp_tools imports) — the file load belongs to the app, not the dataclass. (3) tests/mcp/test_wc_client.py: test_missing_env_raises_keyerror + smoketest missing-env test clear all SEVEN var-names from_env accepts (WC_BASE_URL/WORDPRESS_URL/WP_SITE_URL + WC_/WOOCOMMERCE_ key+secret). (4) tests/test_wc_credentials_from_env.py: dropped the now-dead setattr stub of the removed helper; the delenv loop is the entire isolation. Verified: PYTEST_EXIT=0 (25/25 across both WC test files), ruff clean on all 4 files; pushed da73ed5ab..7bfce75cf fast-forward; re-confirmed on origin/main (helper=0, stub=0, all-7-cleared, bootstrap load present).

root_cause: Snapshot-pinned tests rotted vs evolving SoT. asset_hub froze the 2026-06-25 manifest counts + hardcoded SKU verdicts; manifest since promoted to 73 verified/20 pending. wc_client from_env() auto-loaded symlinked .env.wordpress, defeating monkeypatch.delenv.

error_message: br-011 (Jersey Series 4 Hockey) has garment_type_lock='hoodie' but is a jersey per registry+_JERSEY_SKUS
root_cause: Jersey membership in 3 unsynced places (CSV garment_type_lock, registry sku_folders, _JERSEY_SKUS). br-011 CSV=hoodie, other two=jersey. Same redundant-field disease as br-014/15.
fix: RESOLVED (2026-06-24, commit 9ea639a744e70e638052bab6f7bb32d7258b1f75) — this note was stale. The jersey roster is derived from logo-registry.json sku_folders (scripts/sync_catalog_downstream.py:_registry_jersey_skus), NOT from CSV garment_type_lock, by deliberate design documented in cerebrum.md:79. br-011's garment_type_lock='hoodie' is CORRECT and dossier-confirmed (data/dossiers/black-is-beautiful-jersey-series-4-the-rose-hockey.md: 'Hockey-style pullover hoodie ... with a hood'; 'this is a fashion hoodie, not a tied-collar hockey jersey'). Flipping CSV to 'jersey' changes the render prompt — garment_type_lock is fed verbatim into the RAS/FLUX prompt (skyyrose/elite_studio/agents/three_d_agent.py:95-112, literal f-string 'GARMENT LOCK: {dossier["garment_type_lock"]}') and into the dual-vision QA gate (skyyrose/elite_studio/graph/nodes/ghost_mannequin.py:139-147) — so this was investigated and rejected. Re-open only if the founder explicitly wants to revisit the hood-vs-no-hood render decision; do not re-derive the roster from garment_type_lock again (twice-rejected per cerebrum.md:79).

error_message: Glyphs built from potrace-vectorized PNG cells rendered upside-down (PIL ImageFont render showed an inverted A) despite a sane-looking bbox
root_cause: potrace's SVG <g transform="translate(...) scale(...)"> repositions/scales the path on the page but does not convert SVG's y-down convention into font y-up/baseline-up space -- bbox math looked correct in both orientations so a coordinate sanity check could not have caught it; only rendering the actual glyph and reading the pixels surfaced the flip.
fix: Added a second, explicit Transform().scale(1, -1) after potrace's own translate/scale, plus Cu2QuPen(reverse_direction=True) for correct hole winding on O/A/B/g. Verified by rendering 8 test glyphs (straight lines, curves, holes, descender) with PIL.ImageFont and reading the PNG pixels.

error_message: build_font() crashed with a bare AssertionError deep in fontTools, or silently mis-mapped glyphs, on malformed/ambiguous manifests
root_cause: No validation of manifest cell uniqueness (duplicate char/codepoint -> glyphOrder/glyf length mismatch -> fontTools AssertionError), no bbox bounds checking (OOB bbox silently read as black-padded ink via Image.crop), and Image.open() had no decode-error wrapping (PIL.UnidentifiedImageError leaked past the CLI's narrow except tuple as a raw traceback). Found by an adversarial code-review workflow agent and reproduced.
fix: Added _validate_manifest() (dup char/codepoint, missing keys, OOB bbox -> all raise clear ValueError) called before any processing; wrapped Image.open() to normalize decode failures to ValueError. Verified all 4 new fail-loud paths raise ValueError (not a crash, not silent corruption) with a standalone test script.

error_message: OpenAI gpt-image-2 images.edit() call returns 400 'The model gpt-image-2 does not support the input_fidelity parameter' (code invalid_input_fidelity_model)
root_cause: OAIImageClient.edit() (client.py:74-85) unconditionally sends input_fidelity=config.INPUT_FIDELITY ('high', config.py:37) on every edit() call. config.py's comment claims this is 'verified via Context7... supported for gpt-image-1.5+ (incl. gpt-image-2)' but the LIVE API rejects it for gpt-image-2 specifically -- reproduced 24/24 times in a real call. Context7 (openai/openai-openapi, images/edits schema) lists input_fidelity support for gpt-image-1 / gpt-image-1-mini / gpt-image-1.5 only -- gpt-image-2 is not in that list. This means every product-render edit() call through this client is currently failing the same way, separate from any font-generation work.
fix: FIXED 2026-06-30: config.py defines INPUT_FIDELITY_SUPPORTED_MODELS = {gpt-image-1, gpt-image-1-mini, gpt-image-1.5} (Context7-verified against openai-openapi images/edits schema); client.edit() only sends input_fidelity when config.MODEL is in that set (scripts/oai_render/client.py:88). gpt-image-2 calls omit the param — no more 400s. Buglog entry previously stale ('NOT YET FIXED'); corrected 2026-07-12.

error_message: Skyy mascot (position:fixed) rendered 13,084px down the document instead of the viewport corner
root_cause: pageEnter/pageExit page transitions run on <body> with translateY keyframes + fill-mode:forwards — permanent identity transform on body. Per CSS spec (MDN containing-block), ANY transform (even identity), filter, perspective, will-change:transform, or contain:paint on an ancestor makes it the containing block for fixed descendants.
fix: Opacity-only pageEnter/pageExit keyframes + do-not-reintroduce comment. Ship gate: assert getComputedStyle(document.body).transform === 'none' in live verification.

error_message: pytest teardown noise: 'Future exception was never retrieved' from tests/test_utils_modules.py::test_deduplicate_exception_propagates
root_cause: deduplicate() sets the failure exception on the shared pending future for duplicate callers; when no duplicate caller ever awaits it, the exception is unretrieved at GC and asyncio logs the warning.
fix: Consume the exception immediately after set_exception() via shared_future.exception() (marks it retrieved); primary caller still re-raises. Commit 9f468f298.

error_message: Mascot GLB renders as white untextured silhouette on Safari-path loaders on skyyrose.co live site
root_cause: skyy-v6-web.glb has extensionsRequired: [EXT_texture_webp, KHR_draco_mesh_compression] with baseColor/emissive texture stored as image/webp. Loaders that don't support EXT_texture_webp (Safari-path) drop the required extension and fail to render the texture, producing a white silhouette. Separately, the task spec's build command `gltf-transform optimize ... --texture-compress jpeg` does not exist in gltf-transform CLI v4.4.1 -- the `optimize` subcommand's --texture-compress only accepts ktx2|webp|avif|auto|false, never jpeg/png (confirmed via `optimize --help`); jpeg/png conversion is a separate top-level `jpeg`/`png` subcommand.
fix: Rebuilt from uncompressed master skyy-v6-merged.glb (image/png source, no draco, no webp) using a 3-stage single-pass pipeline instead of the invalid one-shot optimize flag: (1) `gltf-transform resize --width 2048 --height 2048` on the raw PNG texture, (2) `gltf-transform jpeg --formats '*' --quality 92` to convert to universal image/jpeg (alphaMode is OPAQUE so no alpha loss), (3) `gltf-transform draco` applied LAST as a single clean geometry compression pass (running draco before texture ops caused a 'Decoded KHR_draco_mesh_compression, further compression will be lossy' warning on re-encode -- avoided by ordering texture ops before the only draco pass). Result: extensionsRequired=[KHR_draco_mesh_compression] only, no EXT_texture_webp anywhere, image/jpeg 2048x2048, all 6 animation clips (idle/joy/point/talk/walk/wave) and the single skin + JOINTS_0 preserved byte-identical keyframe counts to the source master. 1.44MB total, under the 3.5MB budget. Visually QC'd the extracted JPEG texture atlas at quality 92 -- no banding on flat black/white fills.

error_message: Founder-reported: mascot body posture 'not human-like' -- hunched torso, head pitched down, arms hanging forward, one foot lifted -- visible in bind pose and specifically in the idle loop as played live.
root_cause: Three compounding mistakes across attempts, each caught before reaching production: (1) A prior diagnosis agent's corrective quaternion for the Hips bone ('world +X axis, +8.7954deg') did not reproduce in a fresh Blender session -- applying it moved the lean in the WRONG direction. Root cause: `pose_bone.rotation_quaternion` is defined in the bone's own LOCAL rest-space, not world space. This rig's Hips edit-bone rest orientation is heavily skewed from world axes (euler ~[-9,17,-99] degrees, confirmed via `bone.matrix_local.to_euler()`), so any 'world axis' rotation vector passed directly into `Quaternion(axis, angle)` and assigned to `.rotation_quaternion` was actually being interpreted in that skewed local frame, producing wildly wrong real-world rotations (one attempt collapsed the spine below the hips; another laid the whole body flat on its back). (2) The correct fix -- rotating a bone by a true WORLD-space angle -- requires manipulating `pose_bone.matrix` (armature/world-space) directly: `pb.matrix = Translation(pivot) @ Matrix.Rotation(angle, 4, world_axis) @ Translation(-pivot) @ pb.matrix.copy()`, then letting Blender back-solve the local values. This was verified with a monotonic, reproducible sweep (world X axis, -12.5deg drives Hips->Head world-space lean from -12.62deg to -0.12deg). (3) Even with the correct world-space rotation, applying it via `bpy.ops.pose.armature_apply()` (bake pose as new rest) had ZERO visible effect on the deployed mascot, because `skyy-3d.js` only ever plays named animation clips (idle/walk/etc) via GLTFLoader -- the bind/rest pose is never displayed. `armature_apply()` is explicitly designed to preserve existing animation clips' visual appearance relative to the new rest, so it structurally cannot un-hunch a clip whose own keyframes are the source of the hunch. The idle clip's Hips bone had only 2 keyframes (frames 0 and 72, the loop start/end) baked with the original bad rotation.
fix: Directly re-keyed the idle action's Hips rotation_quaternion at both existing keyframes (frame 0 and 72) using the verified world-space rotate-around-pivot method (`pose_bone.matrix` manipulation, NOT `rotation_quaternion` assignment, NOT `armature_apply()`). Verified via direct render of the re-exported, re-optimized (resize->jpeg->draco) GLB's actual idle clip at 0/25/50/75/100% of the loop -- upright, symmetric, level head, loop closes cleanly. Walk/talk/joy clips were already correctly authored (untouched, confirmed still good); wave/point show an intentional forward-leaning gesture pose, not the standing-hunch defect, left as-is. Result file: renders/3d/mascot/v2/skyy-v8-web.glb, 827KB, all 6 clips + single skin + JOINTS_0/WEIGHTS_0 preserved, draco+jpeg applied. NOT YET deployed to production -- pending founder confirmation per STOP-AND-SHOW.

error_message: Founder: still not fixed why is this so complicated -- its the torso, waste, legs her whole bottom half (after v1.9.5 Hips-lean deploy)
root_cause: Independent of the Hips-lean fix (bug-195), the mascot GLB has a pre-existing crossed-leg base pose (LeftUpLeg/RightUpLeg exaggerated adduction) baked into every one of the 6 animation clips (idle/walk/talk/point/wave/joy) at their starting keyframes, causing the pants mesh to pinch into a visible diagonal crease at the crotch/pelvis. Confirmed present in the pristine original v6-merged.glb, so not introduced by any fix tonight.
fix: idle clip only: re-keyed LeftUpLeg/RightUpLeg rotation_quaternion at both existing keyframes (frames 0, 72) with a world-space Y-axis rotation of -15 degrees (left) / +15 degrees (right), computed via pose_bone.matrix world-space manipulation with the pose frozen (animation_data.action = None) before editing so the edit is not immediately overwritten by animation evaluation on the next depsgraph update. Verified clean via fresh render of the exported result. walk/talk/point/wave/joy were NOT fixed: a -8 to -30 degree sweep of the same world-Y correction against walk frame 1 (much deeper hip flexion than idle) never cleared the crease, indicating a different root cause at high flexion angles (likely a skin-weight pinch at the Hips/UpLeg boundary, not adduction/crossing) that requires mesh/weight-level work, not an animation-keyframe fix. Those 5 clips were left completely untouched in the shipped file (byte-identical to original v6 data) rather than shipping an unproven correction.

error_message: Follow-up to bug-196: walk/talk/point/wave/joy crotch crease at high hip flexion -- three independent fix approaches (weight-smoothing, flexion-angle-clamping, manual weight redistribution) attempted via a mascot-skin-weight-fix Workflow with adversarial verification. All three failed.
file: renders/3d/mascot/v2/skyy-v6-merged.glb (investigated in worktree mascot-skin-weight-fix, no changes shipped)
root_cause: NOT a weight-painting defect -- the Hips/UpLeg vertex weight blend is smooth, anatomically sane, with zero hard cutoffs (0 hard-single vertices out of ~369 in the crotch region). Root cause is Linear Blend Skinning (candy-wrapper collapse) at extreme, stylized hip-flexion angles: these mascot poses hit 126-138 degrees of hip flexion (bind-pose-relative), well beyond real human ROM (~90-120 degrees). At that flexion, binding a vertex 100% to Hips vs 100% to the thigh bone produces deformed positions 26-28% of torso-reference-length apart -- no reweighting between the same two rigid bone transforms can close a gap that large; any blend, narrow or wide, produces visible pinching under LBS math. Confirmed independently by 3 separate diagnostic/fix attempts converging on the same conclusion.
fix: NONE SHIPPED -- all three attempted fixes were adversarially verified (independent re-render + pixel-diff, not self-report) as no-improvement: (1) weight-smoothing via Jacobi diffusion on the Hips/UpLeg/UpLeg vertex groups, both mild (953 verts) and aggressive (1630 verts, 30 iterations) passes -- zero visible change, confirmed via determinism-controlled pixel diff; (2) flexion-angle clamping (halving the excess above 80% of each clip's own peak angle) -- adversarial matched-crop pixel diff disproved the builder's own claimed improvement on talk, AND this approach has a real cost: on this rig flexion is measured from bind pose not neutral-standing, so the threshold caught 102/102 walk frames, nearly halving the stride's rotational amplitude and making the walk animation visibly less dynamic for zero benefit; (3) manual vertex-weight redistribution with a wider, anatomically-anchored (0.5x thigh-bone-length) smoothstep blend band -- independent 800%-zoom + ImageMagick fuzz-diff re-render showed the actual pixel differences were a 1-2px anti-aliasing shift, not a real fold reduction. Recommended next step (not yet attempted): a corrective shape key / morph target sculpted for high hip-flexion, driven by a per-clip morph-weight keyframe track peaking at each clip's known peak frame (walk=86, talk=15, point=59, wave=27, joy=12) -- glTF + three.js natively support morph targets with animated weights, this is the standard fix for LBS candy-wrapper collapse. Fallbacks in order: helper/twist bone between Hips and UpLeg, then dual-quaternion skinning (requires custom three.js runtime/shader work, highest cost).

error_message: Corrective shape-key fix for mascot crotch-fold defect (skyy-fix-shapekey-merged.glb) SHIP-BLOCKING: independent verifier found the baked morph-target rest weight is 1.0 (not 0.0), which three.js GLTFLoader loads directly into mesh.morphTargetInfluences at load time; skyy-3d.js never touches morph targets afterward (clip switching is AnimationMixer fadeIn/fadeOut crossfades only), so the wave-only correction permanently leaks into idle/walk/talk/point/joy in any real browser session. Worst regression in "point" (hip rose-embroidery shatters into jagged triangulated debris, uglier than the original wave defect). Corrects bug-198's scope claim: only "wave" has a genuine LBS candy-wrapper defect at peak flexion — walk/talk/point/joy are smooth/defect-free, confirmed independently by 4 separate methods (raw glTF JSON parse, fresh Blender reimport, three.js source + skyy-3d.js runtime grep, empirical re-render at true baked default).
root_cause: Builder set the shape key's per-clip evaluated value to 0 for undriven clips only inside its OWN verification render harness before rendering — masking that the Key datablock's REST/basis default (what actually gets baked into glTF meshes[0].weights on export) was left at 1.0. Self-graded build report claimed "value permanently 0/unauthored" for the other 4 clips; this was false for the shipped file.
fix: NOT YET SHIPPED — do not deploy skyy-fix-shapekey-merged.glb under any circumstance. One-line fix identified but not yet applied+verified: explicitly set key_blocks["CrotchFoldCorrective_wave"].value = 0.0 on the Key datablock before the glTF export call, then assert meshes[0].weights in ([0],[0.0],None) by parsing the exported GLB's JSON directly (never trust Blender UI state or exporter defaults). Revision agent that was applying this crashed mid-stream (API stream stall) before completing re-verification — workflow resumable via cached run wf_c02081fb-534.

error_message: Revised mascot shape-key build (skyy-fix-shapekey-revised-merged.glb) fixes bug-214's ship-blocking cross-clip leak (idle/walk/talk/point/joy independently confirmed pixel-identical to pristine, correctly carrying flat 0.0 weights) but only wave actually received a correction, and even that is partial (residual angular faceting still visible at f44 per tight zoomed diff). Final verify: overall_verdict="partially-improved", recommend_ship=false, packaged=null.
root_cause: Workflow-design issue, not a code bug: every agent in this lineage (build, verify, revise) was fed the SAME static prompt asserting the original premise ("walk/talk/point/wave/joy" all defective) with an explicit "do not re-litigate" instruction — even after an EARLIER stage in this same lineage independently proved (4 separate methods: raw glTF weights-channel parse, fresh Blender reimport, three.js/skyy-3d.js runtime grep, empirical pristine-vs-candidate render diff) that only wave shows a genuine LBS candy-wrapper defect; walk/talk/point/joy show smooth, normal garment folding at peak flexion with no jagged collapse. The final verify stage flags those 4 clips as a "ship-blocking gap" purely because they don't match the task's original all-5 instruction, not because fresh evidence shows they need fixing. This is the same class of failure as bug-198's scope claim: a stale premise baked into a prompt outliving the evidence that overturned it, this time propagating across a workflow RESUME rather than across separate sessions.
fix: NOT SHIPPED. Recommend treating wave-only as the correct scope (per the well-corroborated independent finding), evaluate whether wave's partial improvement (smoother crease, residual angularity at f44) is worth shipping on its own merits, and do NOT chase further "fixes" for walk/talk/point/joy without first re-deriving — with fresh eyes, ignoring the original task premise — whether those clips are actually defective at all.

error_message: [SECURITY HIGH] Admin MCP bridge (inc/mcp-bridge.php) used a denylist that fails OPEN: it blocked devskyy_lora_generate but not its paid siblings devskyy_lora_pose_transfer/_upscale/_clean_background or devskyy_train_lora_from_products (2-4h GPU job). A compromised manage_options session could invoke them via the free-form AJAX console and spend real Replicate credit under the shared MCP_SERVICE_TOKEN — defeating the control's stated purpose.
root_cause: Denylist model for a money guard: any paid tool not explicitly listed is invocable, so every new/renamed paid tool leaks until someone remembers to add it. Found by adversarial code-review (ran on Sonnet 5 after a fable->sonnet subagent-model downgrade).
fix: Inverted to a fail-closed ALLOWLIST: skyyrose_mcp_authorize_tool() permits a tool only if NOT in the hard blocked list AND (read-only per source readOnlyHint census, 47 tools, OR in the skyyrose_mcp_allowed_write_tools operator filter). All 42 non-read-only tools — every paid generation + every write + any new/untagged tool — are refused by default. Verified: php -l clean, phpcs clean, 12/12 behavioral cases (paid blocked, reads allowed, unknown blocked).

error_message: [SECURITY HIGH] The bug-218 allowlist fix sourced its safe set from readOnlyHint alone, which means 'does not mutate' — NOT 'free'. Six read-only tools cost money and were wrongly allowlisted: devskyy_email_triage + devskyy_analyze_spreadsheet (unconditional live Claude Agent SDK runs, unbounded spend), devskyy_dashboard_health (claude-sdk endpoint), rag_query/rag_query_rewrite (LLM query-variation/HyDE generation), rag_get_context (remote-embedding-capable). A compromised admin could still spend via these.
root_cause: Conflated readOnlyHint (mutation axis) with cost. readOnlyHint:True tools can still call paid third-party LLM/embedding APIs. Found by an adversarial re-review (requested on Fable) that traced tool implementations to the live ClaudeSDKClient / embedding-engine calls.
fix: Allowlist membership rule tightened to read-only AND no external paid/LLM call. Removed the 6 cost-bearing tools from skyyrose_mcp_readonly_tools(); hard-blocked the 2 unbounded agent-run tools (email_triage, analyze_spreadsheet) in skyyrose_mcp_blocked_tools(). Local-ML read tools (demand_forecast/fraud_assess/ml_prediction/recommend/retention_assess) verified to have no paid-API signature and kept. Verified: php -l + phpcs clean, 16/16 behavioral cases (6 cost-bearing now blocked, 7 free reads allowed, 3 paid still blocked).

error_message: [SECURITY MEDIUM] SSRF TOCTOU in inc/fastapi-client.php skyyrose_see_is_safe_url(): it validated the target via gethostbyname() then wp_remote_post/get re-resolved DNS independently at connect time. An admin who set fastapi_url (or mcp_url; the guard is shared with mcp-bridge.php) to a rebindable domain could serve a public IP at check time and an internal IP (Fly 6PN, 169.254.169.254) at connect time, bypassing the private-range filter. Admin-gated, so bounded.
root_cause: Validate-by-hostname then fetch-by-hostname leaves a DNS check-time/connect-time window. Both admin-settable URLs (fastapi_url, mcp_url) legitimately point ONLY at DevSkyy infra, so arbitrary hosts were never a needed feature — that flexibility WAS the attack surface.
fix: Approach A (host allowlist, chosen over connection-pinning because it is transport-agnostic on WP.com and removes the arbitrary-host surface entirely). Added shared skyyrose_see_allowed_backend_hosts() (api.devskyy.app, devskyy-backend.fly.dev, devskyy-api.fly.dev, localhost, 127.0.0.1; filterable) checked in is_safe_url BEFORE any resolution — a non-allowlisted/rebindable host is refused outright, closing the TOCTOU by construction. Private/reserved-range checks retained as defense-in-depth. Covers both callers (fastapi POST/GET + MCP bridge).

error_message: Kids Capsule teaser wordmark ("Kids Capsule") renders with overlapping/illegible glyphs on live page; DOM span rects do NOT overlap (paint-layer artifact, not layout).
root_cause: rv-split-word wraps the h1 into per-word .sr-word spans that carry filter:blur(0) even at REST (.is-visible, animations-premium.css:143). A non-none filter (incl blur(0)) forces a per-span compositing layer, so the background-clip:text gradient painted on the PARENT h1 is read through filter-isolated children -> doubled/overlapping ink on correctly-placed boxes. Structurally pre-existing; the Playfair->Archivo swap (bolder/wider) likely made the latent artifact visible.
fix: Swapped the reveal class rv-split-word -> rv-clip-up on teaser.php:38. rv-clip-up rests with clip-path+opacity and NO filter, and acts on the h1 itself (no child spans) -> clip-mask and glyphs stay on one paint op. Consistent with siblings (.kc-teaser__badge already uses rv-clip-up, tagline uses rv-blur). rv-clip-up is already in the built .min (css+js) so no rebuild needed.

error_message: Kids Capsule teaser hero content (badge+wordmark+tagline) 100% invisible on live prod after v1.10.2 deploy — opacity:0 stuck via inline style, both mobile+desktop.
root_cause: Two reveal systems collided on .kc-teaser__hero-content children. kids-capsule-launch.js:82 gsap.from(children,{opacity:0,y:40}) with immediateRender snapshots each child CURRENT opacity as the tween target; the children carried CSS reveal classes (rv-clip-up/rv-blur) that rest at opacity:0, so GSAP captured 0 and tweened 0->0, leaving inline opacity:0 that outranks the later .is-visible reveal -> permanently invisible. bug-224 fix (rv-split-word->rv-clip-up on the h1) newly pulled the WORDMARK into this same race (previously rv-split-word only zeroed child .sr-word spans, not the h1, so GSAP captured 1 for the h1). Badge+tagline were ALREADY invisible pre-deploy (pre-existing, same race).
fix: Removed the CSS reveal classes (rv-clip-up on badge+wordmark, rv-blur on tagline) from the 3 hero children in teaser.php, making GSAP the SOLE reveal owner: elements default to CSS opacity:1, gsap.from tweens 0->1 visibly; reduced-motion path early-returns in initAnimations() so those users get instant opacity:1. Also eliminates the original overlap bug (no rv-split-word span-splitting). One coherent change; no JS/.min change.

error_message: Landing-page loader stuck at 99% forever; page content never revealed (all first-pass QC screenshots showed loader)
root_cause: Loader progress used lerp-toward-target (p += (target-p)*0.12) with completion check Math.floor(p)>=100 — lerp asymptotically approaches 100 so floor never reaches it
fix: Replaced lerp with time-based eased progress (k=elapsed/DUR, cubic ease-out) and finish on k>=1

error_message: Hero headline/eyebrow/CTAs invisible on desktop+mobile; hero section inflated to 2291px (2.7x viewport)

error_message: re.error: bad escape \U — wolf_recurring_sync passed generated block to re.sub as replacement template; any backslash sequence in a buglog message (Windows path, regex snippet) crashes the sync, and the Stop hook's output suppression made it a silently frozen digest
root_cause: re.sub replacement strings interpret \ escapes (\g<...>, \1). Dynamic text must never be passed as the repl argument directly.

error_message: Mascot crotch 'candy-wrapper' LBS fold in wave/walk/talk/point/joy clips; 3 prior same-night fix attempts (weight smoothing, flexion clamp, manual-blend, 3 shape-key iterations) all failed or regressed (v3's closed-form shape key SHATTERED the wave crotch geometry into jagged radiating facets, worse than pristine).
file: renders/3d/mascot/v2/skyy-fix-shapekey-merged.glb (built fresh from pristine skyy-v6-merged.glb, not chained on any failed attempt)
root_cause: Two distinct bugs conflated as one: (1) SCOPE error — the task assumed all 5 gesture clips needed a fix, but fresh pristine renders (tight crotch-zoom, both math-measured and given peak frames) showed walk/talk/point/joy have smooth, defect-free crotch fabric at peak flexion; only 'wave' (crossed-leg pose, held for its ENTIRE 129-frame duration, not a narrow peak) shows a genuine sharp angular pinch. (2) The prior v3 attempt's 'shattered geometry' was a scale-unit misdiagnosis at the time — mesh/armature world scale is 0.01 (cm-to-m), so a local-space shape-key delta of ~0.94 units (which looks huge vs a 0.257 world-space torso reference) is actually only ~0.0094 world units, i.e. correct and stable; a true closed-form LBS inverse (A_blend.inverted() @ desired_world) is fine as long as both numeric gates (forward-formula matches depsgraph; reapplying A_blend to corrected rest reproduces the desired posed target) are checked in the SAME unit space. Separately, re-exporting the pristine file through Blender's NLA_TRACKS round-trip introduced a spurious +1/24s (1-frame) time-axis shift on the 'idle' clip only, because idle's NLA strip (frame_start=1) doesn't match its action's own frame numbering (action_frame_start=0) — every other clip's strip/action numbering already agree at 1, so only idle was affected. (ID note: renumbered from bug-218 — main independently consumed the 218-230 range.)
fix: Rebuilt end-to-end from pristine only. Measured true peak frames via a closed-form LBS divergence metric (100%-Hips vs 100%-dominant-UpLeg bind, mean-top-50 across ~367 crotch vertices) instead of trusting given peak_frames blindly; validated the closed-form skinning formula against Blender's own depsgraph-evaluated positions (error ~1.7e-7, machine precision). Identified the actual folding vertex subset (top-60 by posed-vs-rest Laplacian delta, +104 halo neighbors at 0.4 falloff weight) instead of the whole 367-vertex crotch region. Computed ONE corrective shape key ('CrotchFoldCorrective_wave') via the closed-form LBS inverse with both numeric gates passing (gate2 error 2.3e-16), targeting a neighbor-averaged smoothing of the actual posed fold at wave's true peak (frame 27). Because the crossed-leg defect pose is held for the wave clip's full 1-129 frame range (verified by rendering 12+ frames spanning the whole clip, not just near the peak), keyframed the shape key value flat at 1.0 across the entire clip rather than a narrow 0->1->0 ramp — measured per-clip, not assumed. Left walk/talk/point/joy/idle completely untouched (no shape key, no Key NLA track). Fixed the idle NLA strip's frame_start/frame_end (1->0, 73->72, matching its action's own 0-72 numbering) purely as an export re-basing correction — zero idle keyframe VALUES touched — which made idle's exported time axis exactly match pristine (0.0 diff, vs +0.041667s before the fix). Verified via gltf-transform inspect + raw glTF JSON/accessor parsing: all 6 clips present, mesh carries exactly 1 morph target (CrotchFoldCorrective_wave), wave's animation channel list includes rotation/translation/scale (bone) channels AND a weights channel targeting the mesh node, the other 4 gesture clips have zero weights channels (fully untouched), and per-clip max animation-value diff vs pristine (~0.0007 quaternion units, ~0.04°) is IDENTICAL in magnitude across touched (wave, idle) and untouched (walk/talk/point/joy) clips — proving it's inherent Blender round-trip float32 requantization, not content drift from this fix. Eyes-on render comparison (tight crotch-zoom, pristine vs fixed, at peak±2/±5 and a 12-frame full-clip bracket) confirms the sharp jagged facet pinch is replaced by a smooth single fold with no popping anywhere in the ramp.

error_message: Phase 3 retargeting-compatibility gate (gate_bone_direction.py) FAILED with any_critical_bone_failed=true before mascot-walk-to-girl-rig skin weighting; per locked plan fork, this mandates skipping the retarget smoke test entirely and pivoting to Plan B (fresh keyframed walk cycle authored directly on the girl rig).
root_cause: girl rig rest pose and skyy.glb mascot rest pose diverge far beyond the locked per-bone-category angle thresholds on CRITICAL bones: Hips 97.4 deg (10 max), Spine 34.0 deg (10 max), neck 43.2 deg (10 max), Head 23.4 deg (10 max), LeftArm 22.5/RightArm 22.3 deg (10 max), LeftForeArm 126.2/RightForeArm 130.5 deg (10 max, near-inverted), LeftLeg 11.8 deg (10 max). 15/24 bones failed overall. Local-space Copy Rotation retargeting assumes near-matching rest poses; a >120 deg forearm rest-direction mismatch would map into inverted-elbow geometry once mascot walk-clip local rotations are copied onto the girl armature. (ID note: renumbered from bug-219 — main independently consumed the 218-230 range.)
fix: No retarget smoke test built (fork rule: Phase-3-failed already satisfies the STOP condition on its own, independent of any smoke-test outcome -- running the smoke test could only ever downgrade a pass, never upgrade this fail). Decision: abandon Local-Space Copy Rotation retargeting for this rig pair. Pivot to Plan B -- author a fresh walk cycle directly on the girl own rig via 8-16 hand-placed pose keys (contact-L, passing-R-up, high-point, contact-R, passing-L-up, high-point, back to contact-L), skipping retargeting entirely.

error_message: First self-check of skin_weight_body.py's own verification pass reported hip/shoulder/armpit_gusset boundaries as FAIL (non-monotonic weight transition) even though the underlying weighting formula (smoothstep on an axis projection) is monotonic by construction.
root_cause: Two distinct bugs, both in the VERIFICATION sampling, not the weighting math: (1) several bones are written by TWO independent, unrelated pathways (e.g. Hips gets weight from both the spine-chain's own Hips<->Spine02 internal joint AND the leg-chains' hip branch blend; Shoulder from both the arm-chain branch blend AND the Phase-2 gusset) -- sorting ALL vertices with nonzero weight on a bone by an unrelated axis projection mixes two populations and breaks monotonicity even though each pathway is independently monotonic. (2) Even after restricting to the correct chain/segment, verification for a branch joint (hip, shoulder) still included vertices already inside the NEXT internal joint's own blend band (e.g. thigh vertices already blending into the knee), whose partial weight on the root bone comes from that unrelated joint, not the hip. A follow-on idempotency check then surfaced a third issue: gusset_verts was recomputed each run as 'len(v.groups)>0', which silently widens to ALL vertices after the first run and corrupts the persisted marker verification depends on. (ID note: renumbered from bug-220 — main independently consumed the 218-230 range.)
fix: classify_all_vertices() recomputes the SAME nearest-chain-polyline assignment the weighting pass used, and every verify_* function filters its sample population by that assignment (chain_name, and for branch joints, seg==0 AND s below the next joint's blend band) instead of by 'bone X has nonzero weight'. gusset_vertex_indices is now persisted as a custom ID property on the mesh ONCE (read back, not recomputed, on subsequent runs) so a second run is a true no-op: 0 newly weighted verts, all 4 boundaries still verify PASS.

error_message: AttributeError: 'Action' object has no attribute 'fcurves' when reading back the walk-cycle source/baked action's F-curves for the location-channel audit.
root_cause: Blender 5.1.2 uses the new layered-Action data model (Animation 2.0 slotted actions): F-curves no longer live at the flat `action.fcurves` used pre-5.0. They are nested under `action.layers[*].strips[*].channelbags[*].fcurves`, one channelbag per animation slot (slot.identifier e.g. 'OBGirlArmature'). High-level APIs like `pose_bone.keyframe_insert()` still work unchanged and auto-create the layer/strip/channelbag structure, but any code that reads F-curves back directly (verification/audit scripts, action introspection) must walk the new nesting -- `action.fcurves` raises AttributeError, it does not silently return an empty list. (ID note: renumbered from bug-221 — main independently consumed the 218-230 range.)
fix: Added `iter_action_fcurves(action)` helper in bake_walk_retarget.py that flattens `action.layers -> strip.channelbags -> fcurves` (filtering strips to type=='KEYFRAME') into a single iterable, matching the pre-5.x flat-list contract. All F-curve consumers (source-action location sanity check, baked-action location-leak audit, fcurve counts) route through this helper instead of `action.fcurves`. Verified via a fresh reload+re-parse of the saved .blend in a separate Blender process.

error_message: raise GateFailure(msg, metric=..., value=..., threshold=...) would crash with 'got multiple values for argument metric'
root_cause: @dataclass decorating an Exception subclass replaces Exception's own __init__ with a dataclass-generated one; the first positional arg (the message string) then binds to the first declared field (metric) instead of being treated as the exception message, colliding with the explicit metric= keyword. (ID note: renumbered from bug-223 — main independently consumed the 218-230 range.)
fix: Removed @dataclass from GateFailure; wrote an explicit __init__(self, message, *, metric, value, threshold) that calls super().__init__(message) and sets the three attributes directly.

error_message: tex_bytes assigned twice in write_rigged_glb: a dead call to _glb_io.image_bytes() with a nonsensical index expression, immediately overwritten by a hand-inlined duplicate of the same slicing logic
root_cause: Draft-writing error while assembling write_rigged_glb() -- wrote the buffer-view slice manually before remembering _glb_io.image_bytes() already implements it, leaving the first assignment as dead code with a confusing `x and 0 or 0` expression. (ID note: renumbered from bug-224 — main independently consumed the 218-230 range.)
fix: Replaced both lines with a single `tex_bytes = _glb_io.image_bytes(clean, blob, 0)` call.

error_message: example character.yaml included z_hip: 0.0 / z_chest: 0.0 / z_head: 0.0 as 'auto-detect' placeholders, which would silently override real detected landmarks with zero
root_cause: landmarks.detect_landmarks() treats KEY PRESENCE in config.landmark_overrides as the override signal (overrides.get('z_hip', auto_detected_value)), not the value -- including a key with a placeholder 0.0 unconditionally uses 0.0 instead of falling through to auto-detection. (ID note: renumbered from bug-225 — main independently consumed the 218-230 range.)
fix: Removed the z_hip/z_chest/z_head placeholder keys entirely from the example yaml; only crotch_y/neck_y (real measured values) are pinned, everything else auto-detects.

error_message: verify_character() FAILED: look.below_neck_max_disp=0.06023 (<= 0.002 required) -- chest-height verts moved 6cm under a pure Head rotation
root_cause: Head/HeadTop_End carry an oversized radius (0.40m, sized for the afro) in the radius-normalized capsule weight solve (w = 1/(distance/radius)^4). Every other bone group (arm, leg) has an explicit region-mask gate confining its influence; Head never got one -- its large radius let it out-compete much-closer, small-radius Spine bones for real chest/torso vertices far below the neck (dn=distance/radius made a farther-but-bigger-radius bone win over a closer-but-smaller one). This is a pre-existing gap in the ORIGINAL validated reference algorithm (rig_girl.py has the identical mask structure), not something the port introduced -- it only surfaced because this was the first time any test ran the pipeline against real, complex geometry instead of a small synthetic mesh. (ID note: renumbered from bug-226 — main independently consumed the 218-230 range.)
fix: Added an explicit region-mask gate in weights.py's _region_masks(): Head/HeadTop_End may only influence vertices at or above the neck joint's Y position (allowed[:, is_head_only] &= (y >= neck_y)), mirroring the existing pattern for arm/leg bone gating. Verified on the real fixture: all 13 WS6 gates now pass (previously 1 failed); confirmed no regression across the existing 24 synthetic-mesh unit tests.

error_message: read_accessor silently returns garbage vertex data on interleaved (byteStride) bufferViews — no exception, wrong geometry
root_cause: np.frombuffer(blob, dtype, count, offset) assumes tight-packed attribute data; glTF legally allows attributes to share a strided bufferView (byteStride set, e.g. POSITION+NORMAL interleaved). GLBWriter (the module's own writer) never interleaves, so no round-trip test could catch it — the exposure is entirely externally-produced GLBs (FBX2glTF output, user passthrough input). Found by python-reviewer agent during /code-review. (ID note: renumbered from bug-227 — main independently consumed the 218-230 range.)
fix: read_accessor now checks bv.byteStride: when set and != tight element size, gathers per-element byte windows via uint8 fancy indexing (start + arange(count)*stride + arange(tight)) before the dtype view. TDD: 3 new tests in tests/character_pipeline/test_glb_io_interleaved.py written RED first (interleaved positions, interleaved normals, tight-stride-set fast-path equivalence).

error_message: clean.py baked normals with the linear part of the node transform instead of its inverse-transpose — wrong-direction normals under any non-uniformly-scaled ancestor node, no error raised
root_cause: n' = M @ n is only direction-correct for rotations/isotropic scale. clean.py's own selling point is composing the ACTUAL node TRS chain (including matrix-form nodes), which explicitly admits non-uniform scale — where the correct transform is the normal matrix inv(M[:3,:3]).T. Failure is invisible in geometry gates (positions unaffected); it manifests only as broken lighting at runtime. Found by python-reviewer agent during /code-review. (ID note: renumbered from bug-228 — main independently consumed the 218-230 range.)
fix: _bake_transform now uses np.linalg.inv(lin).T (identical behavior for the rotation-only case every real asset so far uses — golden fixture regenerated byte-identical, sha256-confirmed); singular transform raises PipelineError. TDD: tests/character_pipeline/test_clean_normals.py written RED first, with a hand-derived 45deg-plane/Y-squash case where naive and correct transforms tilt in OPPOSITE directions. Bonus: RED run exposed a hand-derivation error in my own rotation-case expectation (Rx(-90) maps +z to +y, not -y) — the code was right, the test draft was wrong.

error_message: Three failure modes escaped devskyy-character's documented clean-error contract (nonzero exit + report.json + stderr message), crashing with raw tracebacks instead: landmark auto-detection failures (ValueError x4 sites), FBX2glTF converter failures (CalledProcessError, with the converter's stderr swallowed by capture_output), and bad/malformed --config paths (FileNotFoundError/yaml.YAMLError before the try block)
root_cause: cli.py's except clause only caught PipelineError, but three real failure paths raise other types: landmarks.py raised bare ValueError (a failure mode PROVEN to happen — crotch-detection fails on real clothed-mesh noise, bug-226 session); subprocess.run(check=True, capture_output=True) raises CalledProcessError whose str() omits the captured stderr entirely; and load_character_yaml ran before the guarded region. Found via convergent evidence: my own review pass (ValueError), security-reviewer (CalledProcessError), python-reviewer (CalledProcessError + config-load placement). (ID note: renumbered from bug-229 — main independently consumed the 218-230 range.)
fix: landmarks.py's 4 raise sites converted to PipelineError (same import pattern segment.py already uses); convert.py wraps CalledProcessError into PipelineError surfacing the decoded stderr tail; cli.py moved config load inside the try and widened the except to (PipelineError, FileNotFoundError, yaml.YAMLError).

error_message: setup_character_pipeline_vendor.sh fetched executables with no integrity verification (no checksum pinning) and no curl --fail (HTTP error pages written to disk as 'binaries' with exit 0)
root_cause: GitHub release assets can be replaced under an existing tag and raw.githubusercontent tag refs are movable via force-push, so a URL pin alone is not an integrity guarantee for binaries the pipeline chmod +x's and executes (FBX2glTF). Separately, curl without -f treats an HTTP 404 body as a successful download, so set -euo pipefail never fires and the corruption surfaces later as an opaque exec error. Found by security-reviewer agent during /code-review (MEDIUM + LOW). (ID note: renumbered from bug-230 — main independently consumed the 218-230 range.)
fix: Added per-file sha256 pins (derived 2026-07-10 from official upstream URLs; FBX2glTF is archived upstream and publishes no official checksums) verified after every fetch AND on the skip-existing path, mismatch deletes the file and exits 1; curl now -fsSL. Verified: all 5 pins green against real files, mismatch branch red-tested to exit 1.

error_message: Vercel check fails (red) on all open PRs — appeared to be a systemic main-config break blocking the whole merge queue
file: tasks/pr-vercel-diagnosis.md (finding); Vercel project settings (root cause, not in repo)
root_cause: FALSE BLOCKER. main's Vercel Production deploy is GREEN. 7/8 failing PRs touch 0 frontend files, so their Next build == main's. Identical code can't build-fail on Preview while passing on Production -> the red is a Vercel Preview-environment artifact (Preview-scoped env var missing for @t3-oss/env-nextjs build-time validation, and/or Deployment Protection), NOT PR code and NOT a main-config break.
fix: No repo/code change fixes it. For non-frontend PRs, treat Vercel-red as noise and gate on the Python/test suite instead. To silence preview red: Vercel dashboard -> Settings -> Env Vars (add missing vars to Preview scope) and/or Deployment Protection. frontend/.env.local VERCEL_TOKEN is empty placeholder so vercel inspect can't auth locally.

error_message: Every POST /approval/sync re-uploaded ALL previously-synced approved items — wordpress_synced_at/wordpress_media_id never persisted after successful upload (unbounded duplicate media on live WP library; media linkage lost)
root_cause: sync_item uploaded via sync_approved_asset but never called ApprovalQueueManager.update_wordpress_sync — the only setter of the fields sync_approved_items filters on. Existing test manually called update_wordpress_sync itself instead of pinning that the sync path does it, which is exactly how the gap survived. Found by rev-python during range review, empirically reproduced (2 uploads across 2 runs).
fix: sync_item now persists wordpress_media_id via update_wordpress_sync after a successful upload; a failed state-update converts the result to FAILED (never silent success with lost linkage). Tests: sync-state persistence + run-twice idempotency (upload await_count == 1) + fixture alignment so items live in the manager like production.

error_message: freshness-guard STALE: readme.txt=1.10.3 while style.css/functions.php=1.11.0; then blanket sed bumping readme.txt renamed the '= 1.10.3 =' CHANGELOG heading to '= 1.11.0 =', mislabeling the prior release's notes
root_cause: Theme version lives in THREE files (style.css, functions.php, readme.txt 'Stable tag') and readme.txt contains historical version strings in its changelog; a global sed on the version number cannot distinguish the Stable-tag field from changelog headings. (ID note: renumbered from wolf_bug_id.py's bug-227 — PR #736's branch already claims bug-227..230.) (ID note: renumbered from bug-231 at merge — main claimed it first.) (ID note: renumbered from bug-246 at merge — main claimed it first.)
fix: Restored the '= 1.10.3 =' heading, added a real '= 1.11.0 =' changelog entry above it. Rule: bump versions with targeted replacements (Stable tag line only) and always add a changelog entry; freshness-guard check 3 enforces the three-file sync.

error_message: Built a PR-merge plan on a prior-session audit; treated #672/#684/#686/#689 as open needing conflict resolution. Reality: #684/#686/#689 already MERGED, #672 CLOSED (superseded by #691, also closed). Resolved conflicts on the CLOSED #672 branch (wasted); nearly dispatched resolver agents for 3 already-merged PRs.
root_cause: Stale cross-session audit used as current truth without re-verifying live PR state. Compounded by misreading gh `mergeable`: a MERGED or CLOSED PR returns mergeable=UNKNOWN, identical to an open PR GitHub hasn't computed yet — so UNKNOWN was read as 'recomputing after main moved' when it meant 'not open'. Never queried `state`. merge-tree=0-conflicts also misread as 'auto-mergeable' when it meant 'head is already an ancestor of main (merged)'.
fix: Re-baseline against `gh pr list --state open` (authoritative; excludes merged/closed) BEFORE acting on any PR plan. Never infer open/closed from `mergeable`. Single-PR checks MUST include `state`+`mergedAt`. 0 merge-tree conflicts can mean already-merged, not mergeable.

error_message: verify-visual-manifest.py exit 1 — 7 MISSING: 6 double-extension paths (.webp.webp/.svg.svg) + kids-capsule.avif absent; verifier itself orphaned from CI so it failed silently for months

error_message: security/aes256_gcm_encryption.py _initialize_master_key() fails OPEN: with ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY unset it always generated an ephemeral key + warning, even in production — silently making every previously-encrypted value unrecoverable on the next restart.

Regression tests test_missing_key_in_production_fails_closed / test_missing_key_in_dev_uses_ephemeral added to tests/test_security.py (mutation-verified: with the guard disabled the production test fails 'DID NOT RAISE').
