error_message: tests/scripts/test_deploy_theme.py::TestDryRun::test_dry_run_exits_zero -> awk: can't open file .../readme.txt, deploy-theme.sh exit 2 (Stop-hook blocked)
root_cause: preflight_completeness() (bug-252 gate) read style.css/functions.php/readme.txt with bare awk/sed. On a source tree missing one of them, awk/sed exit non-zero and under `set -euo pipefail` the command substitution crashed the script (raw exit 2) BEFORE the intended -z fail-closed handler ran. The minimal test fixture (2-file theme, no readme.txt) tripped it. Secondary: the drift check `[[ A != B || A != C ]]` triggered shellcheck SC2055 (logic correct, but pattern flagged; suite requires clean shellcheck).
fix: Added explicit file-existence pre-check for the three version files -> clean `exit 1` with a precise message; added `2>/dev/null || true` to the awk/sed reads (defense-in-depth for exists-but-unreadable). Rewrote the drift check to the positive `[[ A == B && B == C ]]` form (logically identical, SC2055-clean). Enhanced test fixture into a minimal *deployable* theme (synced version triple + asset floor) so transport tests clear the gate; added TestCompletenessGate (missing-file / drift / asset-floor / skip-bypass).

error_message: SSRF guard bypass: BrandAssetUpload.url validator gated on case-sensitive str.startswith(('http://','https://')); 'HTTP://169.254.169.254/...' skipped validate_url entirely and reached httpx.get() (cloud-metadata/IAM credential theft).
root_cause: str.startswith is case-sensitive; urlparse/httpx normalize the scheme to lowercase, so a case-variant scheme evaded the startswith gate and the SSRF validator was never called. Guard should never be conditioned on a case-sensitive scheme prefix.
fix: Call SSRFProtection().validate_url(v) UNCONDITIONALLY in the field_validator (validate_url itself rejects any scheme outside {http,https} and every internal/metadata host). Matches the already-correct services/ml/gemini_client.py call site. Added TestBrandAssetSsrfGuard (3 hermetic regression tests) in tests/test_api_auth_hardening.py; uppercase-scheme case fails against pre-fix code. Committed 5c1ff539a on fix/api-auth-hardening.

error_message: preflight_completeness() tracked-file check fails OPEN: `done < <(git ls-files)` process substitution never propagates git's exit status, so a corrupt .git/index (crash/disk-full/permissions) yields zero files and the gate prints 'Tracked-file completeness: 0/0 on disk' + passes (exit 0) — the bug-230 fail-open class, in the very gate meant to catch incomplete deploys. Surfaced by /code-review ultracode adversarial workflow (CONFIRMED HIGH).
root_cause: Process substitution `< <(cmd)` runs cmd in a subshell whose exit status is invisible to `set -e`/pipefail; a git failure after `rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree` succeeds (rev-parse doesn't read the index) silently produces an empty stream.
fix: Capture the list with explicit failure handling: `if ! ls_files=$(git -C "$THEME_DIR" ls-files 2>/dev/null); then log_error 'cannot verify tracked-file completeness; refusing to deploy'; exit 1; fi` then loop over `<<< "$ls_files"`. Fails CLOSED on any git error. Reproduced (truncated .git/index) pre/post-fix: before = '0/0 on disk' exit 0; after = clean error exit 1. Also narrowed the step-2 comment to 'SOURCE-tree completeness' (not deploy-artifact) since tar-excluded tracked files legitimately don't ship (#16).

error_message: Founder: mascot 'walks funny' on live skyyrose.co (v1.11.0, Love Hurts Girl body v7). Chrome-devtools MCP eyes-on capture of the live walk cycle plus a deterministic mixer.setTime() scrub through wordpress-theme/skyyrose-flagship/assets/models/skyy.glb's 'walk' clip (duration 1.0417s) found TWO distinct authoring defects: (1) both UpLeg bones (thigh) hold a perfectly identical rotation quaternion across 4-5 consecutive keyframes (~0.167s, 16% of the cycle) at each swing extreme -- LeftUpLeg flat at t=0.375-0.542 (roll unwrapped 201.74deg), RightUpLeg flat at the same window (roll 145.55deg) -- producing a visible freeze/stutter where she reaches full stride and holds before snapping to the next pose, instead of a continuous swing. Confirmed both numerically (pygltflib keyframe dump) and visually (screenshot diff t=0.45 vs t=0.50 inside the window: 4.5% pixel delta = noise; t=0.30 vs t=0.45 crossing INTO the window: 12.0% pixel delta = real motion). (2) LeftArm/RightArm bones ARE keyframed with a ~40deg rotation delta across the cycle, but pitch/yaw stay bit-identical at every keyframe while only roll (the bone's own long/twist axis) changes -- so the authored rotation is a twist around the upper-arm's length, not a shoulder-flexion swing. Six keyframe-driven screenshots spanning the full cycle (t=0.0/0.15/0.30/0.45/0.65/0.80) show the arms in the IDENTICAL hanging-at-sides position in every frame -- zero visible arm counter-swing despite the keyframes existing. Net visual effect: legs step with a stutter-hold, arms stay rigid -- reads as a robotic/broken gait, matching the founder's 'funny' complaint.
file: wordpress-theme/skyyrose-flagship/assets/models/skyy.glb (walk clip; authored via renders/3d/girl-love-hurts/bake_walk_retarget.py against renders/3d/girl-love-hurts/love-hurts-girl-rig.blend, mascot-skin-weight-fix worktree / PR #736 lineage)
root_cause: The Plan-B fresh-keyframed walk (bug-219 decision: retargeting from the old skyy.glb rig failed Phase 3's bone-direction gate, so the walk was hand-authored directly on the girl's own rig as a sparse 6-pose sequence -- contact-L, passing-R-up, high-point, contact-R, passing-L-up, high-point -- then baked via bpy.ops.nla.bake(channel_types={'ROTATION'}) at a fixed sample rate. Two authoring artifacts survived the bake: (a) the source poses at each swing extreme were placed with an intentional or accidental hold/duplicate spacing in the timeline, so the bake emitted several back-to-back keyframes with byte-identical rotation values instead of a single apex keyframe -- LINEAR interpolation between identical values is a flat hold, not a byte error. (b) the arm poses were authored by rotating the LeftArm/RightArm bone around its local roll axis in Blender, but this rig's arm-bone rest orientation puts its long axis on that same local axis (unlike the leg bones, where local roll happens to be the hinge/flexion axis) -- so the same kind of rotation that correctly swings the leg only twists the arm in place.
fix: Diagnosed + fixed same session. renders/3d/girl-love-hurts/fix_walk_dwell.py opens the already-baked 'walk' action in love-hurts-girl-rig.blend, finds each swing bone's rotation_euler[X] fcurve (LeftUpLeg/RightUpLeg/LeftArm/RightArm), and re-interpolates the interior baked keyframes of both dwell spans (frames 9-12 anchored between 8 and 13; frames 21-24 anchored between 20 and 25) as a straight linear ramp between the untouched outer anchors -- frame 13 keeps its authored contact-extreme value and frame 25 keeps its exact equality with frame 1 (loop-closure invariant preserved). No re-authoring, no re-bake, no change to knee/foot channels (already correct) or gait amplitude. Verified two ways: (1) pygltflib dump of the re-exported GLB shows continuous, monotonically-changing quaternion values through both former dwell spans (was bit-identical across 4-5 consecutive keys); (2) browser mixer.setTime() scrub against the corrected GLB (served locally, port 8934) shows a 6.9pct pixel diff between the exact two frames (old frame 9 vs frame 13) that were previously byte-identical (0pct diff) -- now in the same range as genuine motion elsewhere in the cycle (8.1pct for an adjacent real-motion comparison). Corrected artifacts written to NEW files only (love-hurts-girl-rig.walk-dwell-fix.blend, girl-mascot-raw.walk-dwell-fix.glb) -- canonical love-hurts-girl-rig.blend and the theme-served assets/models/skyy.glb were NOT modified; swapping the corrected animation into the production-compressed draco GLB and deploying is a separate STOP-AND-SHOW step pending founder approval (production asset write + deploy).

error_message: awk: newline in string tell me about the ca... at source line 1 (canon-prefetch.sh UserPromptSubmit hook exits non-zero / surfaces as blocked)
root_cause: scan_patterns() passed the user prompt to awk via -v prompt="$prompt". macOS awk (BWK/nawk) rejects a literal newline in a -v assignment (newline in string), so any multi-line prompt (task-notification / XML blocks) crashed awk; set -euo pipefail propagated the failure and the hook exited 2.
fix: Rewrote scan_patterns to match each pattern against the prompt via a here-string into grep -iqE (grep ... <<< "$prompt") instead of awk -v. Prompt is never an argv element, so arbitrary multi-line content is safe. Avoided printf|grep-q (SIGPIPE+pipefail false-fail on match) by using a here-string. Kept bash 3.2 compatibility (no ${var,,}). Also fixed stale 30->33 SKU count in canon-prefetch.sh directive text.

error_message: SIGSEGV (EXC_BAD_ACCESS) 'crashed on child side of fork pre-exec' — 12+ Python crash reports 21:20-21:26 PDT; fork children of pytest die in nw_settings_child_has_forked -> NEFlowDirectorDestroy -> _os_log_preferences_refresh; parent sees subprocess returncode -11
root_cause: macOS 26.4 + CPython 3.14.3: subprocess takes the fork()+exec path whenever close_fds=True (default) because _HAVE_POSIX_SPAWN_CLOSEFROM is False on macOS. When the multi-threaded pytest parent has initialized Apple's Network.framework (armed via _scproxy system-proxy lookup from urllib/requests/httpx), Apple's atfork child handler nw_settings_child_has_forked() calls os_log whose preferences page is invalid in the fork child -> SIGSEGV pre-exec. Apple framework bug surface, not DevSkyy code. DEEPER CHECK 2026-07-15: crash is a RACE (fork landing while another thread updates os_log preferences — 0/400 forks crashed under synthetic arming incl. live nw_path_monitor). Apple DTS (forums thread 737464): fork() after ANY higher-level framework init is unsupported; posix_spawn is the only reliable fix (syscall — atfork handlers never run). Victims identified: tests spawning python3 generators (build-collection-sot.py, gen-collection-hub.py, gen-design-tokens.py) died Signals.SIGSEGV:11.
fix: conftest.py + scripts/ci-local.sh: on darwin set no_proxy='*'/NO_PROXY='*' (setdefault) so getproxies_environment() is non-empty and _scproxy/Network.framework is never consulted in test processes. Verified: urllib.request.getproxies() -> {'no': '*'}. Residual risk: other Network.framework arming paths; robust per-call fix is posix_spawn conditions (close_fds=False, absolute exe, no cwd/preexec_fn). LAYER 2: conftest.py darwin-only Popen.__init__ patch defaults close_fds=False for test subprocesses (safe per PEP 446 — Python fds are CLOEXEC anyway), flipping them onto the posix_spawn path; explicit close_fds/preexec_fn/pass_fds callers untouched. Verified: 2/2 patched calls route through Popen._posix_spawn; tests/collections/test_build_collection_sot.py 6/6 pass. NOTE: worktree checkouts lack the fix until this lands on their branch — post-fix crashes at 22:23-22:25 traced to unfixed worktree collections-scroll-world running the full suite.

error_message: bug-263 follow-up: full pytest suite still SIGSEGV(-11)'d on bare-executable subprocess calls (git/bash) and on cwd= calls even AFTER the close_fds=False conftest.py patch landed. 90 FAILED in a full-suite run despite the layer-2 fix; isolated re-run of each failing test alone passed clean (bug-231 pattern).
file: conftest.py, scripts/ci-local.sh, tests/test_gitignore_claude_tracking.py, tests/test_sot_assets_tracked.py, tests/test_sot_no_adhoc_imagery.py, tests/scripts/test_check_catalog_duplicates.py, tests/scripts/test_nano_banana_alignment.py, tests/test_ml_optional.py
root_cause: close_fds=False is necessary but NOT sufficient for CPython's subprocess module to take the posix_spawn (syscall, atfork-handler-free) path on macOS. subprocess.py:_execute_child's posix_spawn fast-path gate ALSO requires (a) os.path.dirname(executable) truthy -- i.e. the executable must already be an absolute/relative path, not a bare command name resolved via $PATH -- and (b) cwd is None, with NO CHDIR-file-action fallback in this CPython version. subprocess.run(['git', ...]) / ['bash', ...] (the overwhelming majority of calls in this repo) pass a bare name, so os.path.dirname('git') == '' and CPython silently falls back to fork() regardless of close_fds; any cwd=X kwarg blocks the fast path unconditionally. Empirically proved: importing agents.analytics_agent (pulls numpy/scipy/sklearn/torch transitively via .ml_module) is enough to arm a fork-unsafe framework; a subsequent bare subprocess.run(['git','--version']) then returns -11, while the identical call with executable=shutil.which('git') returns 0. asyncio.create_subprocess_exec also routes through subprocess.Popen internally (asyncio.unix_events._UnixSubprocessTransport._start) so it is covered by the same conftest.py patch. multiprocessing.resource_tracker calls _posixsubprocess.fork_exec() directly, bypassing Popen entirely -- unpatchable from conftest.py; it self-heals via relaunch (harmless UserWarning, 0 test failures, confirmed via --junit-xml) but explains a residual 'resource_tracker: process died unexpectedly' warning + a missing final pytest summary line in piped/redirected full-suite runs even on a fully green suite.
fix: conftest.py: layered a THIRD fix onto the existing close_fds=False Popen patch -- resolve a bare args[0]/kwargs['args'][0] to an absolute path via shutil.which() and pass it as the executable= kwarg (Popen still presents the original argv[0] to the child, so behavior is unchanged for callers); skips shell=True (CPython already defaults executable to /bin/sh there) and any call where executable/cwd were already passed explicitly. Also added OBJC_DISABLE_INITIALIZE_FORK_SAFETY=YES (darwin, setdefault) to conftest.py + scripts/ci-local.sh as defense-in-depth for fork sites this file cannot intercept (e.g. resource_tracker) -- confirmed it does NOT fix resource_tracker's own fork (unpatchable, different mechanism) but is free and harmless. For the unpatchable cwd= call sites: tests/test_gitignore_claude_tracking.py, tests/test_sot_assets_tracked.py, tests/test_sot_no_adhoc_imagery.py switched cwd=ROOT + bare 'git' to git's own -C <dir> flag (matching tests/sparse_guard.py's already-safe pattern); tests/scripts/test_check_catalog_duplicates.py and test_nano_banana_alignment.py dropped an unnecessary cwd= (target scripts resolve all paths via Path(__file__), verified no cwd dependency); tests/test_ml_optional.py's two subprocess.run calls needed cwd=REPO_ROOT for real (load_dotenv()/import resolution), so kept cwd=None on Popen and instead prepended an os.chdir(repo_root) preamble to the injected child script. Full suite: 157 FAILED (clean baseline, no bug-263 fix at all) -> 90 FAILED (after cherry-picking bug-263's close_fds fix + bug-257/bug-231 sibling fixes from a concurrent session) -> 0 FAILED / 0 ERROR (after this fix), confirmed via --junit-xml (6579 tests, 80 skipped, exit 0) across two independent runs.
